intro: Rubus clivicola, also known as Himalayan raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the Himalayan region. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Rubus clivicola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
